name: Order condition via generating functions
statement_text:
A linear multistep method (ρ, σ) has order p if and only if

                     ρ(exp(z)) − zσ(exp(z)) = O(z p+1 ).

  Return now to Theorem 410B and replace exp(z) by (1 + z)−1 . It is found
that
              α((1 + z)−1 ) − log(1 + z)β((1 + z)−1 ) = O(z p+1 ),     (410d)

where log(1 + z) is defined only in {z ∈ C : |z| < 1} by its power series

                     log(1 + z) = z − 12 z 2 + 13 z 3 − · · · .

Because both α(1 + z) and log(1 + z) vanish when z = 0, it is possible to
rearrange (410d) in the form given in the following result, which we present
without further proof.
statement_latex:
\begin{theorem}
A linear multistep method $(\rho, \sigma)$ has order $p$ if and only if
\[
\rho(\exp(z)) - z\sigma(\exp(z)) = O(z^{p+1}).
\]

Return now to Theorem~\ref{thm:410B} and replace $\exp(z)$ by $(1 + z)^{-1}$. It is found that
\[
\alpha((1 + z)^{-1}) - \log(1 + z)\beta((1 + z)^{-1}) = O(z^{p+1}), \tag{410d}
\]
where $\log(1 + z)$ is defined only in $\{z \in \mathbb{C} : |z| < 1\}$ by its power series
\[
\log(1 + z) = z - \frac{1}{2}z^{2} + \frac{1}{3}z^{3} - \cdots .
\]

Because both $\alpha(1 + z)$ and $\log(1 + z)$ vanish when $z = 0$, it is possible to rearrange \eqref{eq:410d} in the form given in the following result, which we present without further proof.
\end{theorem}

variables.name: α
variables.definition: α is the generating polynomial for the coefficients α_i of the linear multistep method, typically α(z) = α_k z^k + α_{k-1} z^{k-1} + ... + α_0

variables.name: β
variables.definition: β is the generating polynomial for the coefficients β_i of the linear multistep method, typically β(z) = β_k z^k + β_{k-1} z^{k-1} + ... + β_0

variables.name: ρ
variables.definition: ρ is the characteristic polynomial of the linear multistep method, typically ρ(z) = α_k z^k + α_{k-1} z^{k-1} + ... + α_0

variables.name: σ
variables.definition: σ is the second characteristic polynomial of the linear multistep method, typically σ(z) = β_k z^k + β_{k-1} z^{k-1} + ... + β_0

variables.name: p
variables.definition: p is the order of the linear multistep method

equations.content: α(exp(−z)) − zβ(exp(−z)) = C0 + C1 z + C2 z^2 + ···

preamble: The context involves linear multistep methods for ODEs, represented in two notations: the [α, β] formulation and the traditional (ρ, σ) formulation. The order condition is derived from Taylor expansions of the method's difference equation, relating it to the generating polynomials evaluated at exp(z).

dependencies.name: Order Condition for Linear Multistep Methods
